notes: Most common letter opening (427+ matches). Recipient first, then sender. TL always ends sender name with -ma enclitic.
Opening with two recipients joined by u (and).
Opening with three recipients. Last two joined by u.
Alternative opening (353+ matches). Sender first with um-ma...ma, then recipient.
Formal opening with qi-bi4-ma (72 matches). Same TL structure as from_to but translated as 'Thus X, say to Y'.
76 matches. Same TL as opening_to_from but translated as 'Say to X, thus Y'.
Used after multi-recipient opening to single out one person (78 matches).

tr: {PN1_TR} brings you {AMT_TR} of {COMM_TR} under my seal.
notes: Cargo manifest with carrier and sealed goods (56 matches). ku-nu-ki-a = under my seal.

tr: {PN1_TR} son of {PN2_TR} brings you {AMT_TR} of {COMM_TR} under my seal.
{PN1_TR} brings you {AMT_TR} of {COMM_TR} under our seals.
notes: Cargo under our (plural) seal. ku-nu-ki-ni = under our seals.
Summary line after a cargo list (12 matches). mi-ma a-nim = all this.
Variant with i-ra-di (leads) instead of na-as (brings). Used with donkey caravans.

tr: All this we entrusted to {PN1_TR} and he brings it to you.

notes: Textile ownership within cargo list (139 matches).

tr: {AMT_TR} kutanu being the working capital of {PN1_TR}

notes: Black donkeys in cargo list (70 matches). Standard transport animal.

tr: {AMT_TR} of tin under seals; {AMT2_TR} loose tin.
notes: Tin cargo split between sealed and loose (expense) tin.

tl: {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR sa-ru-pa-am ni-is-ha-su DIRI sa-du-a-su sa-bu-u
tr: {AMT_TR} of refined silver, its import duty added, its transport tariff paid
notes: Silver with tax status (39 matches). ni-is-ha = import duty, sa-du-a = transport tariff.
Basic send instruction. se-bi4-lam = send to me (singular).
Send to us (plural). se-bi4-la-nim = send to us.
Core trade instruction (78 matches). di-na = sell, se-bi4-la-nim = send.

tr: Sell {COMM_TR} and send the silver with {PN1_TR}.
notes: Sell and send via named carrier.

tr: Buy {AMT_TR} {COMM_TR} and send them to me.
buy {AMT_TR} strong textiles and send them to me.
notes: Buy textiles of specific quality. da-nu-tim = strong/heavy.

tr: Seal {AMT_TR} of silver and send it to me.
Send {COMM_TR} with the very first transport.
notes: Send goods with next caravan. is-ti a-li-ki-im pa-ni-e-ma = with the first transport.

tr: entrust {COMM_TR} to {PN1_TR} so he may bring it here.
notes: Entrust goods to named carrier (112 matches). pi-qi-id = entrust.
Instruction to give goods/silver to someone.

tr: Seize {PN1_TR} and make him pay the silver.
notes: Forceful debt collection (24 matches). sa-ab-ta = seize.

tl: ta-ma-la-ki sa tup-pe-a pi-ti-a-ma tup-pa-am sa hu-bu-ul {PN1_TL} se-li-a-ma
tr: open my tablet coffer and take out the tablet of {PN1_TR}'s debt
notes: Instruction to retrieve debt tablet from storage (19 matches).

tl: tup-pe-a pe-te-a-ma ta-ah-si-sa-tim sa su-mi {PN1_TL} la-ap-tu se-bi4-la-nim
tr: open my tablets and send me the memoranda written in the name of {PN1_TR}.
notes: Instruction to find and send specific memoranda.

tr: Do not sell for less than {AMT_TR} shekels.
notes: Price floor instruction. sa-ha-at = below/less than.

tr: Sell at the rate of {AMT_TR} to 1.
if the rate is less than {AMT_TR} shekels to 1
{AMT_TR} of tin are votive offerings for Aššur
notes: Religious trade obligation (56 matches). ik-ri-bu = votive offerings.
Tin for travel expenses (41 matches). qa-tim = hand (loose/available).
Goods smuggled past customs (27 matches). pa-zu-ur-tim = smuggled.
Breakdown from total (99 matches). SA.BA = of that/thereof.

tr: {PN1_TR} son of {PN2_TR} owes me {AMT_TR} of silver.
{PN1_TR} owes me a deposit of {AMT_TR} of silver.

tl: {AMT_TL} GÍN.TA i-na ITU.1.KAM-im a-na 1 ma-na-im si-ib-tam u-sa-ab
tr: he will pay interest at the rate of {AMT_TR} shekel per mina per month.
notes: Interest rate clause (33 matches). Standard rates: 1, 5/6, 1 1/2, 3 shekels.

tl: su-ma la is-qul {AMT_TL} GÍN.TA i ITU.KAM u-sa-ab
tr: If he does not pay, he will add {AMT_TR} shekels interest per month.
His terms are overdue by {AMT_TR} months.

notes: Payment record (21 matches). as-qu-ul = I paid.
Gift/payment record (50 matches). a-di-in = I gave.
Deduction from balance (55 matches). u-sa-hi-ir = I deducted.
Deposit at remote location (45 matches). e-zi-ib = I left.

tr: I lent you {AMT_TR} of refined silver against interest.

tl: {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR sa-ru-pa-am is-tu ha-mus-tim sa {PN1_TL} ITU.1.KAM {MONTH_TL} li-mi-im {EPONYM_TL} a-na si-ib-tim a-di-na-kum-ma
tr: I lent you {AMT_TR} of refined silver against interest, from the week of {PN1_TR}, month {MONTH_TR}, eponymy {EPONYM_TR},
notes: Full dated loan formula with week, month, and eponymy.

tr: Your terms are overdue by {AMT_TR} months.
Do not bother me for {AMT_TR} days.

notes: Naming someone who can testify about a matter.

tr: Here I seized {PN1_TR} and made him pay {AMT_TR} of silver.

notes: Report of goods delivery. ub-la-ni = brought to me.
Debt owed by named person. KI = with/owed by.

tr: He said: "I shall send {AMT_TR} of silver."
The colony imposed a penalty of {AMT_TR} of copper.

notes: Date formula with week and month (8 matches).

tr: You are my brother. Send me {AMT_TR} of silver.
notes: Kinship greeting + request. Literal 'You are my brother' convention.

tr: My dear brother, send me {AMT_TR} of silver.
notes: Kinship greeting + request. Idiomatic 'My dear brother' convention.

tl: a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi4-lam
tr: You are my father, you are my lord. Send me {AMT_TR} of silver.

tl: a-bi a-ta be-lí a-ta {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi4-lam
tr: My dear father, my dear lord, send me {AMT_TR} of silver.
You are my brothers. Send me {AMT_TR} of silver.
My dear brothers, send me {AMT_TR} of silver.

tl: a-hi a-ta be-lí a-ta {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi4-lam
tr: You are my brother, you are my lord. Send me {AMT_TR} of silver.

tl: a-hi a-ta be-lí a-ta {AMT_TL} KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi4-lam
tr: My dear brother, my dear lord, send me {AMT_TR} of silver.

notes: Ventive -lam = toward speaker. Attested: '3 šé-né-en ša si-ni-iš-tim SIG5 šé-bi4-lam' → 'Send me 3 pairs of good women's shoes.'
Non-ventive šé-bi4-il5 = send AWAY. Contrast with šé-bi4-lam (to me). Attested: 'a-na a-bi4-ni šé-bi4-il5' → 'Send it to our father.'
Non-ventive send to place. Attested: 'KÙ.BABBAR lá-ak-nu-uk-ma a-na a-lim{ki} lu-šé-bi4-il5' → 'send (it) to the city.'
Ventive lu-ub-lam = bring HERE. Attested: 'Ku-li-a i-pá-ni-šu lu-ub-lam' → 'Let Kuliya personally bring it here.'

tr: {PN1_TR} brings you {AMT_TR} of {COMM_TR} under my seal.
notes: na-áš-a-kum = brings to you (ventive toward addressee). Attested: 'lu-ub-lam 4 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ki-a Ú-ku na-ší' → 'Uku brings 4 minas of tin under my seal.'
